# tsb_cluster (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Short name of the Cluster resource.
- `parent` (String) The parent id for the Cluster resource.

### Optional

- `config_generation_metadata` (Attributes) Default metadata values that can be used for configuring segmentation membership When using YAML APIs like `tctl` or `gitops`, put them into the `metadata.labels` or `metadata.annotations` instead. This field is only necessary when using gRPC APIs directly. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config_generation_metadata))
- `description` (String) A description of the resource.
- `display_name` (String) User friendly name for the resource.
- `install_template` (Attributes) Template to be used to install this TSB cluster in the k8s cluster (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template))
- `labels` (Map of String) FIXME: this is super clunky to copy each and every metadata field into objects used for multicluster.
- `locality` (Attributes) Deprecated. For backward compatibility, still honoured but will be ignored in future releases, so better not to set it. Locality of the service endpoints will be dynamically discovered by the xcp-edge Location information about the cluster which can be used for routing. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--locality))
- `namespace_scope` (Attributes) Configure the default scoping of namespaces in this cluster. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--namespace_scope))
- `namespaces` (Attributes List) TODO(vikas): move this inside cluster state Read-only data for informational purposes. Any user provided value will be ignored. The data here may be stale depending on the update frequency from the Bridge agents in the cluster. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--namespaces))
- `network` (String) The network (e.g., VPC) where this cluster is present. All clusters within the same network will be assumed to be reachable for the purposes of multi-cluster routing. In addition, networks marked as reachable from one another in SystemSettings will also be used for multi-cluster routing.
- `state` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--state))
- `tier1_cluster` (Attributes) Deprecated: This flag is still honored for backward compatibility but will be ignored in future releases. It is advisable not to set it, as all clusters can now host both Tier1 and IngressGateways. Indicates whether this cluster is hosting a tier1 gateway or not. Tier1 clusters cannot host other gateways or workloads. Defaults to false if not specified. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tier1_cluster))
- `token_ttl` (Attributes) Lifetime of the tokens. Defaults to 1hr. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--token_ttl))
- `trust_domain` (String) Trust domain for this cluster, used for multi-cluster routing. It must be unique for every cluster and should match the one configured in the local control plane. This value is optional, and will be updated by the local control plane agents. However, it is recommended to set it, if known, so that multi-cluster routing works without having to wait for the local control planes to update it.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Fully-qualified name of the Cluster resource.
- `service_account` (String) The service account created with permissions to manage the current cluster. The service account is not stored and it is only returned in the `ClusterCreate` response.

<a id="nestedatt--config_generation_metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config_generation_metadata`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Set of key value paris that will be added into the `metadata.annotations` field of the Istio generated configurations.
- `labels` (Map of String) Set of key value paris that will be added into the `metadata.labels` field of the Istio generated configurations.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template`

Optional:

- `helm` (Attributes) valid values.yaml to be used with controlplane helm chart. This field is an alpha API, so future versions could include breaking changes. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm))
- `message` (String) can provide useful information to the user

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm`

Optional:

- `image` (Attributes) Values for the TSB operator image. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--image))
- `operator` (Attributes) Values for the TSB operator application. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator))
- `secrets` (Attributes) Values for the Control Plane secrets. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets))
- `spec` (Attributes) Values for the Control Plane CR spec. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--image"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.image`

Optional:

- `registry` (String) Registry used to download the operator image.
- `tag` (String) The tag of the operator image.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.operator`

Optional:

- `control_plane_mode` (String) ControlPlaneMode is the mode for the control plane Possible values are: - UNSET: Defaults to CONTROL - OBSERVE: The control plane will be in observe mode - CONTROL: The control plane will be in control mode
- `deletion_protection` (String) DeletionProtection is the flag for the deletion protection for the control plane Possible values are: - enabled: The control plane will have deletion protection enabled - disabled: The control plane will have deletion protection disabled
- `deployment` (Attributes) Values for the TSB operator deployment. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment))
- `service` (Attributes) Values for the TSB operator service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Values for the TSB operator service account. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.operator.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) Affinity configuration for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--affinity))
- `annotations` (Map of String) Custom collection of annotations to add to the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `env` (Attributes List) Custom collection of environment vars to add to the container. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--env))
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Custom collection of annotations to add to the pod.
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of replicas managed by the deployment.
- `strategy` (Attributes) Deployment strategy to use. Remove Any when working on https://github.com/tetrateio/tetrate/issues/15885 https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Toleration collection applying to the pod scheduling. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.operator.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.operator.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

- `max_unavailable` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--strategy--rolling_update--max_unavailable))

- `str_val` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--strategy--rolling_update--max_surge--str_val))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--deployment--tolerations"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.operator.deployment.tolerations`

Optional:

- `effect` (String)
- `key` (String)
- `operator` (String)
- `toleration_seconds` (Number)
- `value` (String)



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.operator.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Custom collection of annotations to add to the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--operator--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.operator.service_account`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Custom collection of annotations to add to the service account.
- `image_pull_secrets` (List of String) Collection of secrets names required to be able to pull images from the registry.
- `pull_password` (String) Used along pull username and the provided image registry to generate a Docker config JSON that will be stored as a pull secret.
- `pull_secret` (String) A Docker config JSON to be stored in a secret to be used as an image pull secret. If this secret is provided, it will be included in the operator service account as reference. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/pull-image-private-registry/#create-a-secret-by-providing-credentials-on-the-command-line
- `pull_username` (String) Used along pull password and the provided image registry to generate a Docker config JSON that will be stored as a pull secret.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.secrets`

Optional:

- `cluster_service_account` (Attributes) Cluster service account used to authenticate to the Management Plane. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--cluster_service_account))
- `elasticsearch` (Attributes) Secrets to reach the Elasticsearch. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--elasticsearch))
- `tsb` (Attributes) Secrets to reach the TSB Management Plane. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--tsb))
- `xcp` (Attributes) Secrets to reach the XCP Central in the Management Plane. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--xcp))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--cluster_service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.secrets.cluster_service_account`

Optional:

- `cluster_fqn` (String) TSB FQN of the onboarded cluster resource. This will be generate tokens for all Control Plane agents.
- `encoded_jwk` (String) Base64-encoded JWK used to generate and sign the tokens for all the Control Plane agents.
- `jwk` (String) Literal JWK used to generate and sign the tokens for all the Control Plane agents.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--elasticsearch"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.secrets.elasticsearch`

Optional:

- `cacert` (String) Elasticsearch CA cert TLS used by control plane to verify TLS connection.
- `clientcert` (String) Client certificate for mTLS authentication to Elasticsearch.
- `clientkey` (String) Client private key for mTLS authentication to Elasticsearch.
- `password` (String) The password to access Elasticsearch.
- `username` (String) The username to access Elasticsearch.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--tsb"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.secrets.tsb`

Optional:

- `cacert` (String) CA certificate used to verify TLS certs exposed the Management Plane (front envoy).


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--xcp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.secrets.xcp`

Optional:

- `auto_generate_certs` (Boolean) Enabling this will auto generate XCP Edge certificate if mTLS is enabled to authenticate to XCP Central. Requires cert-manager.
- `edge` (Attributes) Secrets for the XCP Edge component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--xcp--edge))
- `rootca` (String) CA certificate of XCP components.
- `rootcakey` (String) Key of the CA certificate of XCP components.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--secrets--xcp--edge"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.secrets.xcp.edge`

Optional:

- `cert` (String) Edge certificate used for mTLS with XCP Central.
- `key` (String) Key of the Edge certificate used for mTLS with XCP Central.
- `token` (String) JWT token used to authenticate XCP Edge against the XCP Central.




<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec`

Required:

- `hub` (String) TSB container hub path e.g. docker.io/tetrate.
- `management_plane` (Attributes) Configure the management plane to retrieve configuration from. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--management_plane))
- `telemetry_store` (Attributes) Configure the store that TSB will use to persist application telemetry data. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--telemetry_store))

Optional:

- `components` (Attributes) The set of components that make up the control plane. Use this to override settings for individual components. These components assume the following secrets are present: oap-token and otel-token. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components))
- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) Pull secrets can be specified globally for all components, or defined into the `kubeSpec.serviceAccount` of every component if needed. In case both are defined, the most specific one (the one defined at the component) level is used. List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--image_pull_secrets))
- `mesh_expansion` (Attributes) Configure mesh expansion to connect workloads external to Kubernetes to the mesh. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion))
- `mesh_observability` (Attributes) Configure how the mesh should be observed, which observability functionalities should be enabled to observe your registered services in the mesh, and the store properties that TSB will use to persist application observability data like metrics, traces, logs. If omitted, a demo grade mesh observability setting will be configured for your convenience. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_observability))
- `mode` (String) Mode in which the Control Plane is deployed. Defaults to Control.
- `provider_settings` (Attributes) Configures Kubernetes provider specific settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings))
- `tier1_cluster` (Boolean) **DEPRECATED**: This should not be set through Control plane API Instead use TSB Cluster API. Indicates that this cluster is used for tier1 gateways. Tier one clusters can only contain tier 1 gateways. Non-tier1 clusters contain tier2 gateways but not tier 1.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--management_plane"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.management_plane`

Required:

- `cluster_name` (String) The name of the Cluster object that was created in the Management Plane representing this Control Plane cluster.
- `host` (String) Management plane host address (can be hostname or IPv4/IPv6 address).
- `port` (Number) Port management plane is listening on.

Optional:

- `self_signed` (Boolean) Management plane uses a self signed or private TLS certificate. If true, the CA bundle used to verify the MP's TLS certificate must be in a secret `mp-certs` under the key `ca.crt`.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--telemetry_store"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.telemetry_store`

Optional:

- `banyandb` (Attributes) Configure the BanyanDB store that TSB will use to persist application telemetry data. When `embeddedBanyanDB` is enabled in the TSB management plane, the management plane will proxy requests from control plane to the  BanyanDB server, so this configuration will be pointing to the management plane, If you have an existing BanyanDB server running, you can modify this setting to point TSB to the existing BanyanDB server. Select the `BanyanDBSettings` settings to see complete examples. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--telemetry_store--banyandb))
- `elastic` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--telemetry_store--elastic))
- `retention_period_days` (Number) Number of days to retain metrics for. Defaults to 7 days. Should be automatically copied from MP and users don't need to set it.
- `traces_retention_period_days` (Number) Number of days to retain traces for. Defaults to 3 days. Should be automatically copied from MP and users don't need to set it.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--telemetry_store--banyandb"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.telemetry_store.banyandb`

Required:

- `host` (String) BanyanDB host address (can be hostname or IP address).
- `port` (Number) Port BanyanDB is listening on.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--telemetry_store--elastic"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.telemetry_store.elastic`

Required:

- `host` (String) Elasticsearch host address (can be hostname or IP address).
- `port` (Number) Port Elasticsearch is listening on.

Optional:

- `client_cert_auth` (Boolean) Enable client certificate authentication (mTLS) to Elasticsearch/OpenSearch. When enabled, the es-certs secret must contain client.crt and client.key. The secret is generated by `tctl install manifest` from the ElasticSecrets client_certificate and client_key fields.
- `index_prefix` (String) The prefix of the ElasticSearch indices and templates. Defaults to `skywalking`.
- `protocol` (String) Protocol to communicate with Elasticsearch, defaults to https.
- `self_signed` (Boolean) Use Self-Signed certificates. The Self-signed CA bundle and key must be in a secret called es-certs.
- `version` (Number) DEPRECATED: Major version of the Elasticsearch cluster. Currently supported Elasticsearch major versions are `6`, `7`, and `8`.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components`

Optional:

- `aws_controller` (Attributes) Configuration for AWS Controller components. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller))
- `collector` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector))
- `default_kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes default settings for all components. These settings will be merged to all components' settings, only if the component does not define the same setting. In that case, the setting defined at the component level prevails over the global default. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec))
- `default_log_level` (String) The default log level for all components if the per component log level config is not specified. Note that the supported log level for different components can be different.
- `gitops` (Attributes) Configuration for the integration of the Control Plane with Continuous Deployment pipelines. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--gitops))
- `hpa_adapter` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter))
- `internal_cert_provider` (Attributes) Configure the Kubernetes CSR certificate provider for TSB internal purposes like Webhook TLS certificates. This configuration is required for kubernetes version 1.22 and above. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider))
- `istio` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio))
- `ngac` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac))
- `oap` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap))
- `onboarding` (Attributes) Workload Onboarding. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding))
- `rate_limit_server` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server))
- `route53_controller` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller))
- `satellite` (Attributes) Satellite provide load balancing capabilities for data content before the data from Envoy reaches the SPM in Control Plane. When envoy points the address to Satellite, it can load balance the traffic to the SPM service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite))
- `spm_agent` (Attributes) SPM Agent provides traffic monitoring capability for applications in non Service Mesh scenarios. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent))
- `wasmfetcher` (Attributes) Configuration for the WASM Fetcher component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher))
- `xcp` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller`

Optional:

- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. *Note*: AWS controller requires [Service Account for IAM to be created before installing the controller](https://docs.tetrate.io/service-express/getting-started/publish-service/#prepare-externaldns-integration-1). Therefore the Service Account will not be managed by this kubeSpec and all Service Account configuration in this kubeSpec will be ignored. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))
- `required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity.preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution`

Optional:

- `preference` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference))
- `weight` (Number)

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity.preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution.preference`

Optional:

- `match_expressions` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference--match_expressions))
- `match_fields` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference--match_fields))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference--match_expressions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity.preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution.preference.match_expressions`

Optional:

- `key` (String)
- `operator` (String)
- `values` (List of String)


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference--match_fields"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity.preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution.preference.match_fields`

Optional:

- `key` (String)
- `operator` (String)
- `values` (List of String)




<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity.required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution`

Optional:

- `node_selector_terms` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--node_selector_terms))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--node_selector_terms"></a>

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.aws_controller.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--aws_controller--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

Optional:

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector`

Optional:

- `envoy_metric_scraping_selectors` (Attributes List) Selects which workloads should have extended Envoy stats enabled and be scraped by this collector. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--envoy_metric_scraping_selectors))
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec))
- `log_level` (String) Specifies the log level for OTEL collector component. Supported log level: "debug", "info", "warn", "error", "dpanic", "panic", and "fatal".
- `metrics` (Attributes) Controls which metrics the OTel collector drops before exporting. By default, a built-in set of high-cardinality metrics is always dropped. Use this field to add extra exclusions or to disable the built-in defaults. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--metrics))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--envoy_metric_scraping_selectors"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.envoy_metric_scraping_selectors`

Optional:

- `namespace` (String) Enable stats and scraping for gateway proxies in this namespace. Only pods with the `istio` label (i.e. gateways) are scraped; sidecar proxies are excluded.
- `workload_selector` (Attributes) Enable stats and scraping for workloads matching these labels in the given namespace. Use this for fine-grained control over which specific pods are scraped. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--envoy_metric_scraping_selectors--workload_selector))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--envoy_metric_scraping_selectors--workload_selector"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.envoy_metric_scraping_selectors.workload_selector`

Optional:

- `labels` (Map of String)
- `namespace` (String)



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))
- `required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.kube_spec.service_account.image_pull_secrets`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.




<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--metrics"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.metrics`

Optional:

- `disable_default_exclusions` (Boolean) When true, the built-in default metric exclusions are not applied and only the rules in the exclusions field above will be used to drop metrics. Defaults to false (built-in defaults are active).
- `exclusions` (Attributes List) Additional metric exclusion rules applied on top of the built-in defaults. When disableDefaultExclusions is true, only these rules are used. Each rule matches a metric by exact name or RE2 regex pattern. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--metrics--exclusions))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--metrics--exclusions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.metrics.exclusions`

Optional:

- `metric` (Attributes) The metric name to match. Use exact for a specific name or regex for a pattern. Required. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--metrics--exclusions--metric))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--collector--metrics--exclusions--metric"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.collector.metrics.exclusions.metric`

Optional:

- `exact` (String) Exact metric name match, e.g. "workqueue_work_duration_seconds_bucket".
- `regex` (String) RE2 regex pattern, e.g. "^workqueue_.*_bucket//github.com/google/re2/wiki/Syntax.





<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.default_kube_spec`

Optional:

- `account` (Attributes) Settings related to the service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--account))
- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the deployments (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment))
- `job` (Attributes) Settings related to the job or cronjob (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--service))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.default_kube_spec.account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--account--image_pull_secrets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.default_kube_spec.account.image_pull_secrets`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.default_kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.default_kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.default_kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.default_kube_spec.job`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job--container_security_context))
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job--pod_security_context))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.default_kube_spec.job.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--default_kube_spec--job--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--gitops"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.gitops`

Optional:

- `batch_window` (Attributes) When configured, all admission requests will be paused for the configured duration. Once the window interval is closed, all paused admission requests will be sent together to the Management Plane as a single request. Batching of requests is disabled by default and should be enabled only if there is high concurrency and ordering of resources could be an issue. By configuring a batch window the concurrency and ordering issues may be mitigated, although it will introduce a constant latency to all requests of the configured time window. When enabled, it is recommended to use a small value, for example 1 second. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--gitops--batch_window))
- `enabled` (Boolean) The GitOps component is in beta and disabled by default. If Management and Control Planes are installed in the same cluster, Continuous Deployment Integration should only be enabled in one of both planes. However, if the GitOps component is enabled in both planes, only the Control Plane GitOps component will remain enabled. The Management Plane GitOps component will not be enabled, even though it is explicitly enabled.
- `managementplane_request_timeout` (Attributes) The GitOps component performs operations against the management plane through the k8s webhook. This allows configuring the duration of each operation in order to fail early if it takes too much. This value cannot be lower than `webhook_timeout` due to the request being tied to the ones received by the k8s webhook. Format: 1h/1m/1s/1m. Any value <= 0 will be reset to the default value. Default: 25s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--gitops--managementplane_request_timeout))
- `push_mode` (String) Push mode determines how the GitOps component creates resources in the Management Plane. In SYNC mode, TSB K8s resources are validated and pushed to the Management Plane synchronously. This means that if a TSB K8s resource is not accepted by the Management Plane, it will not be stored as a resource in the K8s API. SYNC mode can be useful in scenarios where eventual consistency of resources between K8s and Management Plane could cause problems such as in CI pipelines and testing. SYNC is the default mode. In ASYNC mode, TSB K8s resources are pushed to the Management Plane asynchronously. This means that resource creation does not block the process. ASYNC mode is useful in most cases as it does not require the user to manage dependencies between TSB K8s resources. The system will reconcile in the background to achieve the desired state of objects in the Management Plane and will update the Status subresource to report progress of reconciliation.
- `reconcile_interval` (Attributes) Periodical interval at which the objects will be reconciled after they are successfully synchronized (created, updated, deleted) with the Management Plane. This parameter does not affect retry on unsuccessful operation which are retried with exponential backoff strategy (staring with 3s and max delay 120s). Format: 1h/1m/1s/1ms. A value of 0 disables per-object reconciliation and uses the operator's global interval of 10h. Default: 10m. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--gitops--reconcile_interval))
- `reconcile_request_timeout` (Attributes) The GitOps component performs operations against the management plane internal reconcile loop. This allows configuring the duration of each operation to fail early if it takes too long. Format: 1h/1m/1s/1m. Any value <= 0 will be reset to the default value. Default: 2m. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--gitops--reconcile_request_timeout))
- `webhook_timeout` (Attributes) Timeout that will be set in the k8s gitops webhook resource. Format: 1h/1m/1s/1m. Default: 30s. Allowed values must be between 0s and 30s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--gitops--webhook_timeout))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--gitops--batch_window"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.gitops.batch_window`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--gitops--managementplane_request_timeout"></a>

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years

For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.hpa_adapter`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable the OAP (SkyWalking) HPA adapter component that allows pods to be scaled based on Skywalking metrics. This is disabled by default.
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.hpa_adapter.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.hpa_adapter.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.hpa_adapter.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.hpa_adapter.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))
- `required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

- `weight` (Number)
