<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.hpa_adapter.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.hpa_adapter.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.hpa_adapter.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.hpa_adapter.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--hpa_adapter--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.





<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider`

Optional:

- `cert_manager` (Attributes) Use cert-manager as the internal certificate provider (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager))
- `custom` (Attributes) Use a custom certificate provider that accepts Kubernetes CSR (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--custom))
- `tsb_managed` (Attributes) Use TSBManaged certificates for internal purposes. TSB Operator skips installing cert-manager. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--tsb_managed))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager"></a>

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.cert_manager.cert_manager_ca_injector.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_ca_injector--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_ca_injector--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_ca_injector--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_ca_injector--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_ca_injector--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_ca_injector--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_ca_injector--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_ca_injector--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_spec--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_spec--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_spec--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_spec--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_spec--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_spec--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_spec--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_spec--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.cert_manager.cert_manager_startupapicheck.kube_spec.job`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--job--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--job--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the job. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--job--env))
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--job--pod_security_context))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--job--tolerations))

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.cert_manager.cert_manager_startupapicheck.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_startupapicheck--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.cert_manager.cert_manager_webhook_spec.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

- `int_val` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port--int_val))
- `str_val` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port--str_val))
- `type` (Number)

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port--int_val"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.cert_manager.cert_manager_webhook_spec.kube_spec.service.ports.target_port.int_val`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The int32 value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port--str_val"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.cert_manager.cert_manager_webhook_spec.kube_spec.service.ports.target_port.str_val`

- `value` (String) The string value.





<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.cert_manager.cert_manager_webhook_spec.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--cert_manager--cert_manager_webhook_spec--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.cert_manager.cert_manager_webhook_spec.kube_spec.service_account.image_pull_secrets`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.






<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--custom"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.custom`

Required:

- `ca_bundle_secret_name` (String) Configure the CABundleSecretName to be used to verify the signed CSR request by different TSB components. If not specified, TSB would use the secret with the name ca-bundle-management-plane in the management plane namespace or ca-bundle-control-plane in the control plane namespace. The secret should contain the file ca.crt with the cert data.
- `csr_signer_name` (String) Name of Kubernetes CSR signer to be used to sign the CSR request by different TSB components for internal purposes.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--internal_cert_provider--tsb_managed"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.internal_cert_provider.tsb_managed`



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio`

Optional:

- `ambient` (Attributes) Ambient configuration for Istio (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient))
- `default_workload_cert_ttl` (Attributes) The default TTL of issued workload certificates. This sets both the default client-side CSR TTL and the default server-side issued certificate TTL. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--default_workload_cert_ttl))
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec))
- `log_levels` (Map of String) Specifies the global logging level settings for the Istio control plane components.
- `max_workload_cert_ttl` (Attributes) The maximum TTL that can be set in issued workload certificates. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--max_workload_cert_ttl))
- `mount_internal_wasm_extensions` (Attributes) When this flag is set to true, the TSB internal WASM extensions will be mounted into the Sidecar, Ingress and Egress gateway pods automatically. These extensions will be loaded as local files instead of being downloaded from a remote OCI registry or HTTP endpoint. This is not enabled by default. Set it to true to enable it. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--mount_internal_wasm_extensions))
- `trace_sampling_rate` (Number) The percentage of traces Envoy will sample.
- `trust_domain` (String) The trust domain corresponds to the trust root of a system. Refer to [SPIFFE-ID](https://github.com/spiffe/spiffe/blob/main/standards/SPIFFE-ID.md#21-trust-domain). If omitted, TSB will configure the trust domain as `CLUSTER_NAME.tsb.local`, where `CLUSTER_NAME` is the name of the cluster object in TSB for this control plane.
- `tsb_version` (String) Specifies the tsb release version. This is used by the tsb control plane operator in determining the xcp version, which would eventually decide Istio version. If not provided explicitly, this defaults to the current tsb version.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient`

Optional:

- `enable` (Attributes) Enable or disable Ambient mode (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--enable))
- `revision` (String) Deprecated: use ztunnel_revision instead. Retained as a backward compatible alias: when `ztunnel_revision` is not set, the value of this field is used as the ztunnel revision.
- `waypoints` (Attributes) Cluster-level waypoint configuration for Ambient mode (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints))
- `ztunnel_revision` (String) Revision of the ztunnel to deploy. ztunnel is a per-node component and only a single ztunnel revision can be active across the cluster at any given time. If not set, it falls back to the deprecated `revision` field, and otherwise defaults to the "default" revision.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--enable"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.enable`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints`

Optional:

- `cluster_level` (Attributes) Cluster-level waypoint configuration. These settings are used if no namespace-level configurations are found. If namespace-level configurations are present, they take precedence. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--cluster_level))
- `namespace_level` (Attributes List) List of namespace-level waypoint configurations. Each entry specifies waypoint settings for a specific namespace or set of namespaces. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--cluster_level"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.cluster_level`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String)
- `disable_label_management` (Attributes) If true, xcp will not apply/remove istio.io/use-waypoint label on/from the namespace of the ambient workloads. Default is false and in that case namespace level waypoint redirection is enabled automatically by xcp. If this is set to true, then user has to manually apply the istio.io/use-waypoint label on the namespace to enable waypoint redirection. Otherwise, traffic will not go through waypoints and will be directly routed to the destination ambient workloads. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--cluster_level--disable_label_management))
- `enable` (Attributes) Enable or disable default waypoint deployment (default is true) This configuration can be made at both the cluster level and also at namespace level. Cluster level configs will be applied in all the onboarded application namespaces which are in the ambient mode and do not have namespace level configs. Example, if user wants waypoint to be deployed in selected few namespaces, then user can disable the cluster level config and enable the namespace level config for those namespaces. If both cluster level and namespace level configs are enabled, then namespace level configs will take precedence. If both cluster level and namespace level configs are disabled, then waypoint will not be deployed in any of the namespaces. If cluster level config is enabled and namespace level config is not configured, then waypoint will be deployed in all the onboarded application namespaces which are in the ambient mode. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--cluster_level--enable))
- `labels` (Map of String) Deprecated: Use overlays field to add labels, annotations, tolerations and resource requirements.
- `overlays` (String) overlays can be used to fully customize waypoint deployments, services and hpa. Example: ```yaml istio:   ambient:     enable: true     waypoints:       clusterLevel:         xfccWasmPluginUrl: file:///wasm-plugins/xcp-guard.wasm         overlays:           istio-waypoint:  <-- This is the waypoint's gateway class name.             deployment:               metadata:                 annotations:                   cluster-level: via-excp-value       namespaceLevel:       - config:           overlays:             istio-waypoint:               deployment:                 metadata:                   annotations:                     via-excp: via-excp-value               horizontalPodAutoscaler:                 spec:                   maxReplicas: 3                   minReplicas: 1         name: echo <-- This is the namespace name. ``` This allows users to customize the waypoint deployments, services and hpa. "clusterLevel" overlays will be applied to all the waypoints deployed by xcp in the cluster. This includes waypoints deployed in app namespaces and also the waypoints deployed in the xcp-multicluster namespace. "namespaceLevel" overlays will be override the "clusterLevel" overlays for the specific namespace. Note that namespace level overlays will totally replace the cluster level overlays for the specific namespace. overlays will be used to generate the `data:` section of the configmap, as documented at  https://istio.io/latest/docs/tasks/traffic-management/ingress/gateway-api/, which is created by xcp for waypoint  customization.
- `resources` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--cluster_level--resources))
- `revision` (String) Istio revision of the waypoint(s) at this scope. Allows upgrading waypoints to a different Istio revision than ztunnel. Multiple distinct waypoint revisions may run concurrently during an upgrade. If unset, a namespace-level waypoint inherits the cluster-level waypoint revision, which itself defaults to the ztunnel revision.
- `tolerations` (Map of String)

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--cluster_level--disable_label_management"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.cluster_level.disable_label_management`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--cluster_level--enable"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.cluster_level.enable`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--cluster_level--resources"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.cluster_level.resources`

Optional:

- `limits` (Map of String)
- `requests` (Map of String)



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.namespace_level`

Optional:

- `config` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level--config))
- `name` (String)
- `selector` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level--selector))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level--config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.namespace_level.config`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String)
- `disable_label_management` (Attributes) If true, xcp will not apply/remove istio.io/use-waypoint label on/from the namespace of the ambient workloads. Default is false and in that case namespace level waypoint redirection is enabled automatically by xcp. If this is set to true, then user has to manually apply the istio.io/use-waypoint label on the namespace to enable waypoint redirection. Otherwise, traffic will not go through waypoints and will be directly routed to the destination ambient workloads. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level--config--disable_label_management))
- `enable` (Attributes) Enable or disable default waypoint deployment (default is true) This configuration can be made at both the cluster level and also at namespace level. Cluster level configs will be applied in all the onboarded application namespaces which are in the ambient mode and do not have namespace level configs. Example, if user wants waypoint to be deployed in selected few namespaces, then user can disable the cluster level config and enable the namespace level config for those namespaces. If both cluster level and namespace level configs are enabled, then namespace level configs will take precedence. If both cluster level and namespace level configs are disabled, then waypoint will not be deployed in any of the namespaces. If cluster level config is enabled and namespace level config is not configured, then waypoint will be deployed in all the onboarded application namespaces which are in the ambient mode. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level--config--enable))
- `labels` (Map of String) Deprecated: Use overlays field to add labels, annotations, tolerations and resource requirements.
- `overlays` (String) overlays can be used to fully customize waypoint deployments, services and hpa. Example: ```yaml istio:   ambient:     enable: true     waypoints:       clusterLevel:         xfccWasmPluginUrl: file:///wasm-plugins/xcp-guard.wasm         overlays:           istio-waypoint:  <-- This is the waypoint's gateway class name.             deployment:               metadata:                 annotations:                   cluster-level: via-excp-value       namespaceLevel:       - config:           overlays:             istio-waypoint:               deployment:                 metadata:                   annotations:                     via-excp: via-excp-value               horizontalPodAutoscaler:                 spec:                   maxReplicas: 3                   minReplicas: 1         name: echo <-- This is the namespace name. ``` This allows users to customize the waypoint deployments, services and hpa. "clusterLevel" overlays will be applied to all the waypoints deployed by xcp in the cluster. This includes waypoints deployed in app namespaces and also the waypoints deployed in the xcp-multicluster namespace. "namespaceLevel" overlays will be override the "clusterLevel" overlays for the specific namespace. Note that namespace level overlays will totally replace the cluster level overlays for the specific namespace. overlays will be used to generate the `data:` section of the configmap, as documented at  https://istio.io/latest/docs/tasks/traffic-management/ingress/gateway-api/, which is created by xcp for waypoint  customization.
- `resources` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level--config--resources))
- `revision` (String) Istio revision of the waypoint(s) at this scope. Allows upgrading waypoints to a different Istio revision than ztunnel. Multiple distinct waypoint revisions may run concurrently during an upgrade. If unset, a namespace-level waypoint inherits the cluster-level waypoint revision, which itself defaults to the ztunnel revision.
- `tolerations` (Map of String)

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level--config--disable_label_management"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.namespace_level.config.disable_label_management`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.namespace_level.config.enable`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level--config--resources"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.namespace_level.config.resources`

Optional:

- `limits` (Map of String)
- `requests` (Map of String)



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--ambient--waypoints--namespace_level--selector"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.ambient.waypoints.namespace_level.selector`

Optional:

- `labels` (Map of String)





<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--default_workload_cert_ttl"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.default_workload_cert_ttl`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `cni` (Attributes) Configure Istio's CNI plugin For further details see: https://istio.io/docs/setup/additional-setup/cni/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--cni))
- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to all the component deployments (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment))
- `istiod_deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the istiod deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment))
- `operator_deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the Istio operator deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--cni"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.cni`

Optional:

- `binary_directory` (String) Directory on the host to install the CNI binary. Must be the same as the environment’s `--cni-bin-dir` setting (kubelet parameter).
- `chained` (Boolean) Whether to deploy the configuration file as a plugin chain or as a standalone file in the configuration directory. Some Kubernetes flavors (e.g. OpenShift) do not support the chain approach.
- `cluster_role` (String) The ClusterRole Istio CNI will bind to in the ControlPlane namespace. This is useful if you use Pod Security Policies and want to allow `istio-cni` to run as privileged Pods.
- `cni_daemonset_namespace` (String) The namespace where the Istio CNI DaemonSet is deployed. Defaults to kube-system in sidecar mode (for backward compatibility on upgrades) and istio-operator namespace in the ambient mode.
- `configuration_directory` (String) Directory on the host to install the CNI config. Must be the same as the environment’s `--cni-conf-dir` setting (kubelet parameter).
- `configuration_file_name` (String) Leave unset to auto-find the first file in the `cni-conf-dir` (as kubelet does). Primarily used for testing install-cni plugin configuration. If set, `install-cni` will inject the plugin configuration into this file in the `cni-conf-dir`.
- `revision` (String) The revisioned istio-operator that will reconcile the Istio CNI component. A revision can only be specified when Isolation Boundaries are enabled and configured with at least one revision. Revision specified here must be an enabled revision under `xcp.isolationBoundaries`. If not provided, it defaults to the latest enabled revision based on their corresponding tsbVersion. If multiple such revisions are found, revision names are alphabetically sorted and the first revision is considered as the default.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.istiod_deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.istiod_deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.operator_deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.operator_deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.operator_deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))
- `required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--istio--max_workload_cert_ttl"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.max_workload_cert_ttl`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.istio.mount_internal_wasm_extensions`
