- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.ngac`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) NGAC is an experimental component. If enabled is false, this component will not be installed.
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec))
- `log_levels` (Map of String) The log level configuration by scopes. Supported log level: "none", "error", "info", "debug".

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.ngac.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.ngac.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.ngac.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.ngac.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.ngac.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.ngac.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.ngac.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--ngac--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap`

Optional:

- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec))
- `log_level` (String) Specifies the log level for OAP component. Supported log level: "all", "debug", "info", "warn", "error", "fatal", "off" and "trace".
- `on_demand_envoy_metrics_enabled` (Boolean) Feature flag to determine whether on-demand envoy metrics should be enabled. If enabled, the envoy proxy will provide a set of metrics that can be queried using the metrics service. OAP will provide a query API that can be used to collect envoy proxy metrics for specific pods. This is only for temporary and real-time queries that can be used, for example, for application troubleshooting use cases. These metrics are not persisted.
- `storage_index_merging_enabled` (Boolean) Feature flag to determine whether metrics/meter and records should be shard into multi-physical indices, or instead if they should be merged into a single physical index. By default "false", metric/meter and records are sharded into multi-physical indices. Instead of sharding, if enabled by setting it to "true", metrics/meter and records will be merged into one physical index template `metrics-all` and `records-all`. This feature flag must be set on all clusters and have the same value as the management plane's one, otherwise control plane observability data could be written to the wrong or not existing index. In this storage mode, user can adjust each concrete index should have to scale out by setting `storageSpecificIndexSettings` field in the management plane install manifest.
- `streaming_log_enabled` (Boolean) Feature flag to determine whether on-demand streaming logs should be enabled.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))
- `required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

- `weight` (Number)

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference"></a>

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap.kube_spec.service.ports`

Optional:

- `name` (String)
- `node_port` (Number)

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.oap.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--oap--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

- `log_levels` (Map of String) The log level configuration by scopes. Supported log level: "none", "error", "warn", "info", "debug".

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.operator.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.operator.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.operator.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.operator.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.operator.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.operator.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--operator--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.plane`

Optional:

- `instance` (Attributes) Kubernetes settings for the `Workload Onboarding Plane Instance` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.plane.instance`

Optional:

- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec))
- `log_levels` (Map of String) The log level configuration by scopes. Supported log level: "none", "error", "warn", "info", "debug".

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.plane.instance.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.plane.instance.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.plane.instance.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.plane.instance.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

- `min_replicas` (Number) Must be set in order to create the HPA resource in Kubernetes

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.plane.instance.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.

- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--plane--instance--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.repository.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--overlays))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.repository.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.repository.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.repository.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.repository.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.repository.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.repository.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.onboarding.repository.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--onboarding--repository--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.






<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.rate_limit_server`

Required:

- `backend` (Attributes) Configure Database backend settings. This field must be configured by the user. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--backend))

Optional:

- `domain` (String) The domain field allows ratelimits to be namespaced to a certain domain. To support common ratelimits across multiple clusters set this string to a common value, across them. This assumes that the same backend (uri) is being used. By default the domain is set to the name of the control plane cluster.
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--backend"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.rate_limit_server.backend`

Optional:

- `redis` (Attributes) Settings for redis database backend. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--backend--redis))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--backend--redis"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.rate_limit_server.backend.redis`

Required:

- `uri` (String) The Redis Database URI. The value of the URI decides the scope for ratelimiting across multiple clusters.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.rate_limit_server.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.rate_limit_server.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.rate_limit_server.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

Optional:

- `kind` (String) Resource kind.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.rate_limit_server.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--service"></a>

- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--rate_limit_server--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.route53_controller`

Optional:

- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. *Note*: Route53 controller requires [Service Account for IAM to be created before installing the controller](https://docs.tetrate.io/service-express/getting-started/publish-service/#prepare-externaldns-integration-1). Therefore the Service Account will not be managed by this kubeSpec and all Service Account configuration in this kubeSpec will be ignored. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.route53_controller.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.route53_controller.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.route53_controller.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.route53_controller.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))
- `required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution"></a>

Optional:

- `preference` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference))
- `weight` (Number)

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference"></a>

Optional:

- `match_expressions` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference--match_expressions))

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.

More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--route53_controller--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))
