<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.satellite`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Satellite is an optional component. If enabled is false, this component will not be installed.
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec))
- `log_level` (String) Specifies the log level for the component. Supported log level: "panic", "fatal", "info", "warn", "error", "debug" and "trace".

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.satellite.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.satellite.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.satellite.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.satellite.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.satellite.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.satellite.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.satellite.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--satellite--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.





<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.spm_agent`

Optional:

- `disable_multi_protocol_analysis` (Boolean) Set to true to disable multiple protocol analysis. Default is false. When disabled, if the detected protocol is HTTP, the TCP protocol data won't be analyzed. When enabled, if the detected protocol is HTTP, both the TCP and HTTP protocols will be analyzed together.
- `enabled` (Boolean) SPM Agent is an optional component. If enabled is true, this component will be installed.
- `host_path` (String) The root directory of each host for SPM agent use. By default, the value is "/". In the case of container-in-container, it is necessary to specify the path of the current host directory within the current container.
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec))
- `log_level` (String) The log level configuration by scopes. Supported log level: "panic", "fatal", "error", "warn", "info", "debug" and "trace".
- `priority` (Attributes) The agent deployment priority setting. It addresses "0/xx nodes are available" scenarios to avoid monitoring failures due to insufficient node resources. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--priority))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.spm_agent.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.spm_agent.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.spm_agent.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.spm_agent.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.

- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--service--ports))

ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--spm_agent--priority"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.spm_agent.priority`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) The agent deploy priority is enabled or not. By default, the value is false.
- `value` (Number) The agent deploy priority value. The value is following the configuration rule in https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/#priorityclass. By default, the value is 1000000 (for high priority).



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher`

Optional:

- `cache_disable_insecure_registries` (Boolean) Denies insecure registries to be used for fetching WASM modules. Defaults to `false`.
- `cache_expiration` (Attributes) WASM Module cache expiration time. Defaults to `24h`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--cache_expiration))
- `cache_max_retries` (Number) Maximum number of retries when fetching WASM modules from the OCI registry. Defaults to `5`.
- `cache_purge_interval` (Attributes) WASM cache purge interval to periodically clean up the stale WASM modules. Defaults to `1h`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--cache_purge_interval))
- `cache_request_timeout` (Attributes) Specifies the timeout used when retrieving the WASM plugin from the OCI registry. Defaults to `15s`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--cache_request_timeout))
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec))
- `log_levels` (Map of String) The log level configuration by scopes. Supported log levels: "none", "error", "info", "debug".

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--cache_expiration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.cache_expiration`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--cache_purge_interval"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.cache_purge_interval`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--cache_request_timeout"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.cache_request_timeout`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--overlays))

- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.kube_spec.service.ports`

Optional:

- `name` (String)
- `node_port` (Number)
- `port` (Number)
- `protocol` (String)
- `target_port` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.wasmfetcher.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--wasmfetcher--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.





<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp`

Optional:

- `central_auth_mode` (String) Authentication mode for connections from XCP Edges to XCP Central. If not set will default to mutual TLS.
- `central_provided_ca_cert` (Attributes) If true, obtain the CA cert for Istio from XCP central. To enable it, the XCP Central needs to be configured with `certIssuer.clusterIntermediateCASettings: {}`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--central_provided_ca_cert))
- `config_protection` (Attributes) ConfigProtection contains settings for enabling/disabling config protection over XCP created resources. Config protections are disabled by default. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--config_protection))
- `delete_direct_mode_translated_resources` (Attributes) Stops the XCP edge translator from emitting the same-namespace translated duplicate of user-applied direct-mode Istio CRs. This is phase two of the direct-mode resource reduction and should only be enabled after enable_reduced_direct_mode_resources is on and the rewrite-istio-rev webhook has been verified to be rewriting CRs end-to-end. This feature is disabled by default. Set it to true to enable it. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--delete_direct_mode_translated_resources))
- `enable_http_mesh_internal_identity_propagation` (Attributes) Enables HTTP mesh internal service identity propagation across gateway hops, utilizing the propagated identity for evaluating TSB RBAC rules. Users should enable this feature when they want to create RBAC rules around request's origin client identity for east west traffic. The most common case for this would be when using authorization features such as ALLOW/DENY rules mode and ServiceSecuritySettings in cross-cluster environment. This feature is enabled by default. Set it to false to disable it. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--enable_http_mesh_internal_identity_propagation))
- `enable_jwt_authentication_require_jwt_token_setting` (Attributes) Enables JWT authentication require JWT token settings via EnvoyFilter with strict validation. Requests without a valid JWT token will be rejected with a 401 (Unauthorized) error. This feature is disabled by default. Set it to true to enable it. Along with this, setting the `require_jwt` field in the authentication settings of the gateway is needed. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--enable_jwt_authentication_require_jwt_token_setting))
- `enable_reduced_direct_mode_resources` (Attributes) Reduces the number of Istio resources created per user-applied direct-mode Istio CR by installing the rewrite-istio-rev mutating webhook (and migration trigger) on the XCP edge, so the customer's CR is consumed by Istio directly instead of via a same-namespace translated duplicate. Only effective when running the TSB Control Plane in GitOps mode. This feature is disabled by default. Set it to true to enable it. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--enable_reduced_direct_mode_resources))
- `isolation_boundaries` (Attributes List) Configures Isolated Istio environments along with Istio revisions for each environment. IsolationBoundaries can be empty when the feature flag IstioIsolationBoundaries is disabled. Once enabled, isolation boundaries can be configured. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries))
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec))
- `log_levels` (Map of String) Loglevel for XCP. Supported log level: "none", "fatal", "error", "warn", "info", "debug".
- `remote_diagnostic` (Attributes) Remote Diagnostic settings on the Control Plane side. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--remote_diagnostic))
- `revision` (String) //istio.io/latest/blog/2020/multiple-control-planes/#configuring Deprecated: replaced by isolation_boundaries, where each isolation boundary can have mulitple revisions.
- `shared_gateway_enabled` (Attributes) Enables the Shared Gateway feature, which allows gateways owned by one workspace to be referenced and used by other workspaces via SharedGatewayReferenceGrant resources. This feature is disabled by default. Set it to true to enable it. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--shared_gateway_enabled))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--central_provided_ca_cert"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.central_provided_ca_cert`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--config_protection"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.config_protection`

Optional:

- `authorized_users` (List of String) List of usernames of authorized users or svc accounts to create/update/delete XCP configs when config protection is enabled.
- `enable_authorized_create_update_delete_on_xcp_configs` (Boolean) When enabled, no other user or svc account except AuthorizedUsers would be allowed to create, delete or update the XCP/Istio API resources. This acts as a superset of the enableAuthorizedUpdateDeleteOnXcpConfigs.
- `enable_authorized_update_delete_on_xcp_configs` (Boolean) When enabled, no other user or svc account except AuthorizedUsers would be allowed to delete or update the XCP/Istio API resources created by XCP.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--delete_direct_mode_translated_resources"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.delete_direct_mode_translated_resources`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--enable_http_mesh_internal_identity_propagation"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.enable_http_mesh_internal_identity_propagation`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--enable_jwt_authentication_require_jwt_token_setting"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.enable_jwt_authentication_require_jwt_token_setting`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--enable_reduced_direct_mode_resources"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.enable_reduced_direct_mode_resources`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the IsolationBoundary.
- `revisions` (Attributes List) Configure multiple Istio Revisions under the IsolationBoundary. Once IstioIsolationBoundaries is enabled, for any IsolationBoundary configured - there must be atleast one IstioRevision. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions))

Optional:

- `mesh_expansion` (Attributes) Configure mesh expansion to connect workloads external to Kubernetes to the mesh. Each IsolationBoundary has its own mesh expansion settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions`

Required:

- `istio` (Attributes) Istio overlay configuration for the revision. Revision specific Istio configs will be overlayed over the common Istio configs configured in the ControlPlaneSpec. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio))
- `name` (String) Name of the IstioRevision. Must be unique at cluster level, across Isolation Boundaries. The IstioRevision name is used to deploy revisioned Istio control-plane components. Notice that the value constraints here are stricter than the ones in Istio. Apparently, Istio validation rules allow values that lead to internal failures at runtime, e.g. values with capital letters or values longer than 56 characters. Stricter validation rules here are meant to prevent those hidden pitfalls.

Optional:

- `disable` (Boolean) If set to `true`, Istio control plane deployment with this revision will be cleaned up from the cluster. This field can be used to clean up revisioned control plane deployment while retaining the configurations in the CR. After cleanup, it can be again set to `false` to re-deploy revisioned control plane. By default the value is set to `false`.
- `gateway_reconciliation` (Attributes) Controls automatic reconciliation of gateway deployments for this revision. When not set, reconciliation is enabled (backward compatible default). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--gateway_reconciliation))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio`

Optional:

- `ambient` (Attributes) Ambient configuration for this revision (enable-only). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--ambient))
- `default_workload_cert_ttl` (Attributes) The default TTL of issued workload certificates. This sets both the default client-side CSR TTL and the default server-side issued certificate TTL. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--default_workload_cert_ttl))
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec))
- `log_levels` (Map of String) Specifies the global logging level settings for the Istio control plane components.
- `max_workload_cert_ttl` (Attributes) The maximum TTL that can be set in issued workload certificates. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--max_workload_cert_ttl))
- `mount_internal_wasm_extensions` (Attributes) When this flag is set to true, the TSB internal WASM extensions will be mounted into the Sidecar, Ingress and Egress gateway pods automatically. These extensions will be loaded as local files instead of being downloaded from a remote OCI registry or HTTP endpoint. This is not enabled by default. Set it to true to enable it. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--mount_internal_wasm_extensions))
- `trace_sampling_rate` (Number) The percentage of traces Envoy will sample.
- `trust_domain` (String) The trust domain corresponds to the trust root of a system. Refer to [SPIFFE-ID](https://github.com/spiffe/spiffe/blob/main/standards/SPIFFE-ID.md#21-trust-domain). If omitted, TSB will configure the trust domain as `CLUSTER_NAME.tsb.local`, where `CLUSTER_NAME` is the name of the cluster object in TSB for this control plane.
- `tsb_version` (String) Specifies the tsb release version. This is used by the tsb control plane operator in determining the xcp version, which would eventually decide Istio version. If not provided explicitly, this defaults to the current tsb version.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--ambient"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.ambient`

Optional:

- `enable` (Attributes) Enable or disable Ambient mode (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--ambient--enable))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--ambient--enable"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.ambient.enable`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--default_workload_cert_ttl"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.default_workload_cert_ttl`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `cni` (Attributes) Configure Istio's CNI plugin For further details see: https://istio.io/docs/setup/additional-setup/cni/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--cni))
- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to all the component deployments (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment))
- `istiod_deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the istiod deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment))
- `operator_deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the Istio operator deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--cni"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.kube_spec.cni`

Optional:

- `binary_directory` (String) Directory on the host to install the CNI binary. Must be the same as the environment’s `--cni-bin-dir` setting (kubelet parameter).
- `chained` (Boolean) Whether to deploy the configuration file as a plugin chain or as a standalone file in the configuration directory. Some Kubernetes flavors (e.g. OpenShift) do not support the chain approach.
- `cluster_role` (String) The ClusterRole Istio CNI will bind to in the ControlPlane namespace. This is useful if you use Pod Security Policies and want to allow `istio-cni` to run as privileged Pods.
- `cni_daemonset_namespace` (String) The namespace where the Istio CNI DaemonSet is deployed. Defaults to kube-system in sidecar mode (for backward compatibility on upgrades) and istio-operator namespace in the ambient mode.
- `configuration_directory` (String) Directory on the host to install the CNI config. Must be the same as the environment’s `--cni-conf-dir` setting (kubelet parameter).
- `configuration_file_name` (String) Leave unset to auto-find the first file in the `cni-conf-dir` (as kubelet does). Primarily used for testing install-cni plugin configuration. If set, `install-cni` will inject the plugin configuration into this file in the `cni-conf-dir`.
- `revision` (String) The revisioned istio-operator that will reconcile the Istio CNI component. A revision can only be specified when Isolation Boundaries are enabled and configured with at least one revision. Revision specified here must be an enabled revision under `xcp.isolationBoundaries`. If not provided, it defaults to the latest enabled revision based on their corresponding tsbVersion. If multiple such revisions are found, revision names are alphabetically sorted and the first revision is considered as the default.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity`

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))
- `required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--required_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.kube_spec.deployment.affinity.node_affinity.preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution`

Optional:

- `preference` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference))
- `weight` (Number)

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution--preference"></a>

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.kube_spec.istiod_deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.kube_spec.istiod_deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--istiod_deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.kube_spec.operator_deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity"></a>

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--operator_deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.

ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--istio--max_workload_cert_ttl"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.istio.max_workload_cert_ttl`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--gateway_reconciliation"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.gateway_reconciliation`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Attributes) Global switch for gateway reconciliation under this revision. When disabled, no gateway deployments for this revision are reconciled unless explicitly overridden at namespace or object level. Default: true (reconciliation enabled, backward compatible). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--gateway_reconciliation--enabled))
- `namespace_overrides` (Attributes List) Per-namespace overrides. Each entry controls reconciliation for all gateways whose application namespace (spec.namespace on the gateway CR) matches. Takes precedence over the revision-level `enabled` setting. A gateway CR's object-level label still takes precedence over these overrides. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--gateway_reconciliation--namespace_overrides))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--gateway_reconciliation--enabled"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.gateway_reconciliation.enabled`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--gateway_reconciliation--namespace_overrides"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.revisions.gateway_reconciliation.namespace_overrides`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Attributes) Whether to enable reconciliation for gateways in this namespace. Overrides the revision-level `enabled` setting. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--gateway_reconciliation--namespace_overrides--enabled))
- `namespace` (String) The application namespace where gateways are deployed. This matches the `spec.namespace` field on gateway CRs (IngressDeployment, EgressDeployment, Tier1Deployment, GatewayDeployment). If spec.namespace is unset on the gateway CR, it defaults to the CR's own metadata.namespace.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--revisions--gateway_reconciliation--namespace_overrides--enabled"></a>
