### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion`

Optional:

- `custom_gateway` (Attributes) A custom mesh expansion gateway. This is required when the workload can't access the default gateway directly via the external IP or hostname. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--custom_gateway))
- `host_management` (Attributes) EXPERIMENTAL: Settings for the `Tetrate Host Management` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management))
- `onboarding` (Attributes) Configuration of the `Workload Onboarding Plane`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding))
- `service_observability` (Attributes) EXPERIMENTAL: Settings for the `Tetrate Service Observability` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--custom_gateway"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.custom_gateway`

Required:

- `host` (String) Mesh expansion gateway host address (can be hostname or IP address).
- `port` (Number) Port mesh expansion gateway is listening on.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.host_management`

Required:

- `endpoint` (Attributes) Configuration of the endpoint exposing `Host Management API` to `Host Management Agents`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--endpoint))

Optional:

- `agent` (Attributes) Settings for the `Tetrate Host Management Agent`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--agent))
- `enabled` (Attributes) To install the component,  set the value to `true`. Defaults to `false`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--enabled))
- `plane` (Attributes) Configure `Tetrate Host Management Plane` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane))
- `repository` (Attributes) Configure `Tetrate Host Management Repository` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--endpoint"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.host_management.endpoint`

Required:

- `hosts` (List of String) List of hosts included in the TLS certificate.
- `secret_name` (String) Name of the secret that holds TLS certificate chain and private key. The secret can be either a TLS secret or a generic one. Keys `tls.crt` and `tls.key` are mandatory. Additionally, the secret may also have a `ca.crt` key to provide the certificate of a custom CA that issued given TLS cert.

Optional:

- `has_no_dns_record` (Attributes) To configure `Agents` to connect to the `Host Management Endpoint` using an address of the k8s Service rather than host name, set the value to `true`. Defaults to `false`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--endpoint--has_no_dns_record))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--endpoint--has_no_dns_record"></a>

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.

- `version` (String) Version of the `Tetrate Host Management Agent` to use. Defaults to the version of the `Tetrate Host Management Agent` included into a given TSB release.

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.

ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.

- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec.service_account.image_pull_secrets`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.






<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding`

Required:

- `endpoint` (Attributes) Configuration of the endpoint exposing `Workload Onboarding API` to `Workload Onboarding Agents`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--endpoint))

Optional:

- `local_repository` (Attributes) Configuration of the local repository with `DEB` and `RPM` packages of the `Workload Onboarding Agent` and `Istio Sidecar`. Local repository is disabled by default. To enable it, set this field to an empty value, i.e. `localRepository: {}`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--local_repository))
- `token_issuer` (Attributes) Configuration of the built-in `Workload Onboarding Token Issuer`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer))
- `uid` (String) Unique identifier of this particular installation of the `Workload Onboarding Plane`. Is used in the workload authentication flow to prevent replay attacks that abuse compromised workload credentials intended for a different installation of the `Workload Onboarding Plane`. Defaults to an auto-generated UUID.
- `workloads` (Attributes) Configuration of the workload handling. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--endpoint"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.endpoint`

Required:

- `hosts` (List of String) List of hosts included in the TLS certificate.
- `secret_name` (String) Name of the secret that holds TLS certificate chain and private key.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--local_repository"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.local_repository`


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.token_issuer`

Optional:

- `jwt` (Attributes) Configuration of the built-in JWT Token Issuer. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer--jwt))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer--jwt"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.token_issuer.jwt`

Optional:

- `expiration` (Attributes) Expiration is the duration issued tokens are valid for. Defaults to `1h`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer--jwt--expiration))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer--jwt--expiration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.token_issuer.jwt.expiration`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years




<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads`

Optional:

- `authentication` (Attributes) Workload authentication configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication))
- `deregistration` (Attributes) Workload deregistration configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--deregistration))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication`

Optional:

- `jwt` (Attributes) JWT authentication configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication.jwt`

Optional:

- `issuers` (Attributes List) List of permitted JWT issuers. If a workload authenticates itself by means of an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken), the issuer of that token must be present in this list, otherwise authentication attempt will be declined. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication.jwt.issuers`

Required:

- `issuer` (String) JWT `Issuer` identifier. The value must be a case sensitive URL using the https scheme that contains scheme, host, and optionally, port number and path components and no query or fragment components. E.g., `https://mycompany.corp`, `https://accounts.google.com`, `https://sts.windows.net/9edbd6c9-0e5b-4cfd-afec-fdde27cdd928/`, etc. See https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken
- `short_name` (String) Unique short name associated with the issuer. The value must consist of lower case alphanumeric characters and hyphen (`-`). Since this value will be included into the auto-generated name of the `WorkloadAutoRegistration` resource, keep it as short as possible. E.g., `my-corp`, `prod`, `test`, etc.

Optional:

- `jwks` (String) Inlined JSON Web Key Set document. Specifies public keys the `Workload Onboarding Plane` should use to validate the signature of an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken).
- `jwks_uri` (String) URL of the JSON Web Key Set document. Source of public keys the `Workload Onboarding Plane` should use to validate the signature of an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken). E.g., `https://mycompany.corp/jwks.json`. When unspecified, URL the JSON Web Key Set document will be resolved using [OpenID Connect Discovery](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html) protocol.
- `token_fields` (Attributes) Description of the custom fields included in the [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken). By default, `Workload Onboarding Plane` interprets only one field that is always present in a valid [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken) - `sub` (subject). If you want `Workload Onboarding Plane` to interpret custom fields included in the [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken), you have to provide an explicit configuration. E.g., you can instruct the `Workload Onboarding Plane` to treat a certain field as a map of fine-grained attributes associated with the subject. It will allow you to define `OnboardingPolicy`(s) that match those attributes. Notice that this description instructs how to interpret custom fields if they are present in an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken). A token in which custom fields are not present is still valid. An `OnboardingPolicy` that does not put constraints on attributes extracted from custom fields can still match a workload with that token. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers--token_fields))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers--token_fields"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication.jwt.issuers.token_fields`

Optional:

- `attributes` (Attributes) Field that carries a map of fine-grained attributes associated with the subject of the [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken). If specified, `Workload Onboarding Plane` will treat the name/value pairs extracted from this field as attributes associated with the workload. It will allow you to define `OnboardingPolicy`(s) that match those attributes. E.g., if an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken) includes the following fields: ```yaml {   "iss": "https://mycompany.corp",   "aud": "ef67c7b9-10da-4542-ad3b-b95acc1e05ba",   "sub": "us-east-datacenter1-vm007",   "azp": "us-east-datacenter1-vm007",   "iat": 1613404941,   "exp": 1613408541,   "custom_attributes": {     "region": "us-east",     "datacenter": "datacenter1",     "instance_name": "vm007",     "instance_hostname": "vm007.internal.corp",     "instance_role": "app-ratings"   } } ``` then, you can indicate to the `Workload Onboarding Plane` to treat the contents of field `custom_attributes` as fine-grained attributes associated with the workload. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers--token_fields--attributes))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers--token_fields--attributes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication.jwt.issuers.token_fields.attributes`

Required:

- `json_path` (String) Simple JSON Path which is evaluated against custom claims of the [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken) to produce the value of the field. E.g., `.custom_attributes`, `.google.compute_engine`, etc. JSON Path must start either from `.` or from `//goessner.net/articles/JsonPath/






<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--deregistration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.deregistration`

Optional:

- `propagation_delay` (Attributes) Estimated amount of time it takes to propagate the unregistration event across all affected mesh nodes. During this time interval affected proxies will continue making requests to the deregistered workload until the respective configuration update arrives. To prevent traffic loss, `Workload Onboarding Agent` SHOULD delay shutdown of the the workload's sidecar for that time period. As a rule of thumb, this value should remain relatively small, e.g. under 15 seconds. The reason for this is that shutdown flow on the workload's side is time-boxed. E.g., on VMs there is a stop timeout enforced by SystemD, while on AWS ECS there is a stop timeout enforced by ECS Agent. If you pick a delay value that is too big, `Workload Onboarding Agent` will delay shutdown of the sidecar for too long; as a result sidecar risks to get terminated abruptly instead of graceful connection draining. Defaults to `10s`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--deregistration--propagation_delay))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--deregistration--propagation_delay"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.deregistration.propagation_delay`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years





<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability`

Optional:

- `agent` (Attributes) Settings for the `SPM Agent`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--agent))
- `domains` (List of String) List of domains to consider when discovering services. Services outside of domains specified here will NOT be added to the registry of services known to TSB and will NOT appear on TSB UI. Empty list means that discovered services may belong to any domain. Defaults to an empty list.
- `enabled` (Attributes) To install the component,  set the value to `true`. Defaults to `false`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--enabled))
- `service_discovery` (Attributes) Configure `Tetrate Service Discovery` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--agent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability.agent`

Optional:

- `version` (String) Version of the `SPM Agent` to use. Defaults to the version of the `SPM Agent` included into a given TSB release.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--enabled"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability.enabled`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery`

Optional:

- `agent` (Attributes) Settings for the `Tetrate Service Discovery Agent`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--agent))
- `plane` (Attributes) Configure `Tetrate Service Discovery Plane` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--agent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.agent`

Optional:

- `version` (String) Version of the `Tetrate Service Discovery Agent` to use. Defaults to the version of the `Tetrate Service Discovery Agent` included into a given TSB release.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane`

Optional:

- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec))
- `log_levels` (Map of String) The log level configuration by scopes. Supported log levels: "none", "error", "info", "debug".

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity"></a>

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.isolation_boundaries.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.

- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--isolation_boundaries--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.kube_spec.service.ports`

Optional:

- `name` (String)
- `node_port` (Number)
- `port` (Number)
- `protocol` (String)
- `target_port` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port"></a>

- `str_val` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port--str_val))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.kube_spec.service_account.image_pull_secrets`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.




<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--remote_diagnostic"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.remote_diagnostic`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Attributes) Enable Remote Diagnostic on the Control Plane side. Once Remote Diagnostic is enabled on the Control Plane side, it will become possible to launch from the TSB UI a range of predefined diagnostic tasks for execution in the context of that cluster. In particular, it will be possible to take config dumps, view low-level metrics, view and change log levels and stream logs from any Istio Gateway and Istio Sidecar deployed to that cluster. Notice that Remote Diagnostic has to be enabled on both sides, i.e. the Control Plane side and the Management Plane side, which is the default configuration. Defaults to `true`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--remote_diagnostic--enabled))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--components--xcp--remote_diagnostic--enabled"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.remote_diagnostic.enabled`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.

### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.components.xcp.shared_gateway_enabled`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.




<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--image_pull_secrets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.image_pull_secrets`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion`

Optional:

- `custom_gateway` (Attributes) A custom mesh expansion gateway. This is required when the workload can't access the default gateway directly via the external IP or hostname. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--custom_gateway))
- `host_management` (Attributes) EXPERIMENTAL: Settings for the `Tetrate Host Management` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management))
- `onboarding` (Attributes) Configuration of the `Workload Onboarding Plane`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding))
- `service_observability` (Attributes) EXPERIMENTAL: Settings for the `Tetrate Service Observability` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--custom_gateway"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.custom_gateway`

Required:

- `host` (String) Mesh expansion gateway host address (can be hostname or IP address).
- `port` (Number) Port mesh expansion gateway is listening on.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management`

Required:

- `endpoint` (Attributes) Configuration of the endpoint exposing `Host Management API` to `Host Management Agents`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--endpoint))

Optional:

- `agent` (Attributes) Settings for the `Tetrate Host Management Agent`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--agent))
- `enabled` (Attributes) To install the component,  set the value to `true`. Defaults to `false`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--enabled))
- `plane` (Attributes) Configure `Tetrate Host Management Plane` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane))
- `repository` (Attributes) Configure `Tetrate Host Management Repository` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--endpoint"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.endpoint`

Required:

- `hosts` (List of String) List of hosts included in the TLS certificate.
- `secret_name` (String) Name of the secret that holds TLS certificate chain and private key. The secret can be either a TLS secret or a generic one. Keys `tls.crt` and `tls.key` are mandatory. Additionally, the secret may also have a `ca.crt` key to provide the certificate of a custom CA that issued given TLS cert.

Optional:

- `has_no_dns_record` (Attributes) To configure `Agents` to connect to the `Host Management Endpoint` using an address of the k8s Service rather than host name, set the value to `true`. Defaults to `false`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--endpoint--has_no_dns_record))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--endpoint--has_no_dns_record"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.endpoint.has_no_dns_record`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--agent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.agent`

Optional:

- `version` (String) Version of the `Tetrate Host Management Agent` to use. Defaults to the version of the `Tetrate Host Management Agent` included into a given TSB release.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--enabled"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.enabled`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.plane`

Optional:

- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec))
- `log_levels` (Map of String) The log level configuration by scopes. Supported log levels: "none", "error", "info", "debug".

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.plane.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.plane.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.plane.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--overlays))
