<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.host_management.repository.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--host_management--repository--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding`

Required:

- `endpoint` (Attributes) Configuration of the endpoint exposing `Workload Onboarding API` to `Workload Onboarding Agents`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--endpoint))

Optional:

- `local_repository` (Attributes) Configuration of the local repository with `DEB` and `RPM` packages of the `Workload Onboarding Agent` and `Istio Sidecar`. Local repository is disabled by default. To enable it, set this field to an empty value, i.e. `localRepository: {}`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--local_repository))
- `token_issuer` (Attributes) Configuration of the built-in `Workload Onboarding Token Issuer`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer))
- `uid` (String) Unique identifier of this particular installation of the `Workload Onboarding Plane`. Is used in the workload authentication flow to prevent replay attacks that abuse compromised workload credentials intended for a different installation of the `Workload Onboarding Plane`. Defaults to an auto-generated UUID.
- `workloads` (Attributes) Configuration of the workload handling. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--endpoint"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.endpoint`

Required:

- `hosts` (List of String) List of hosts included in the TLS certificate.
- `secret_name` (String) Name of the secret that holds TLS certificate chain and private key.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--local_repository"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.local_repository`


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.token_issuer`

Optional:

- `jwt` (Attributes) Configuration of the built-in JWT Token Issuer. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer--jwt))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer--jwt"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.token_issuer.jwt`

Optional:

- `expiration` (Attributes) Expiration is the duration issued tokens are valid for. Defaults to `1h`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer--jwt--expiration))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--token_issuer--jwt--expiration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.token_issuer.jwt.expiration`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years




<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads`

Optional:

- `authentication` (Attributes) Workload authentication configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication))
- `deregistration` (Attributes) Workload deregistration configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--deregistration))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication`

Optional:

- `jwt` (Attributes) JWT authentication configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication.jwt`

Optional:

- `issuers` (Attributes List) List of permitted JWT issuers. If a workload authenticates itself by means of an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken), the issuer of that token must be present in this list, otherwise authentication attempt will be declined. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication.jwt.issuers`

Required:

- `issuer` (String) JWT `Issuer` identifier. The value must be a case sensitive URL using the https scheme that contains scheme, host, and optionally, port number and path components and no query or fragment components. E.g., `https://mycompany.corp`, `https://accounts.google.com`, `https://sts.windows.net/9edbd6c9-0e5b-4cfd-afec-fdde27cdd928/`, etc. See https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken
- `short_name` (String) Unique short name associated with the issuer. The value must consist of lower case alphanumeric characters and hyphen (`-`). Since this value will be included into the auto-generated name of the `WorkloadAutoRegistration` resource, keep it as short as possible. E.g., `my-corp`, `prod`, `test`, etc.

Optional:

- `jwks` (String) Inlined JSON Web Key Set document. Specifies public keys the `Workload Onboarding Plane` should use to validate the signature of an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken).
- `jwks_uri` (String) URL of the JSON Web Key Set document. Source of public keys the `Workload Onboarding Plane` should use to validate the signature of an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken). E.g., `https://mycompany.corp/jwks.json`. When unspecified, URL the JSON Web Key Set document will be resolved using [OpenID Connect Discovery](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html) protocol.
- `token_fields` (Attributes) Description of the custom fields included in the [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken). By default, `Workload Onboarding Plane` interprets only one field that is always present in a valid [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken) - `sub` (subject). If you want `Workload Onboarding Plane` to interpret custom fields included in the [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken), you have to provide an explicit configuration. E.g., you can instruct the `Workload Onboarding Plane` to treat a certain field as a map of fine-grained attributes associated with the subject. It will allow you to define `OnboardingPolicy`(s) that match those attributes. Notice that this description instructs how to interpret custom fields if they are present in an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken). A token in which custom fields are not present is still valid. An `OnboardingPolicy` that does not put constraints on attributes extracted from custom fields can still match a workload with that token. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers--token_fields))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers--token_fields"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication.jwt.issuers.token_fields`

Optional:

- `attributes` (Attributes) Field that carries a map of fine-grained attributes associated with the subject of the [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken). If specified, `Workload Onboarding Plane` will treat the name/value pairs extracted from this field as attributes associated with the workload. It will allow you to define `OnboardingPolicy`(s) that match those attributes. E.g., if an [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken) includes the following fields: ```yaml {   "iss": "https://mycompany.corp",   "aud": "ef67c7b9-10da-4542-ad3b-b95acc1e05ba",   "sub": "us-east-datacenter1-vm007",   "azp": "us-east-datacenter1-vm007",   "iat": 1613404941,   "exp": 1613408541,   "custom_attributes": {     "region": "us-east",     "datacenter": "datacenter1",     "instance_name": "vm007",     "instance_hostname": "vm007.internal.corp",     "instance_role": "app-ratings"   } } ``` then, you can indicate to the `Workload Onboarding Plane` to treat the contents of field `custom_attributes` as fine-grained attributes associated with the workload. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers--token_fields--attributes))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--authentication--jwt--issuers--token_fields--attributes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.authentication.jwt.issuers.token_fields.attributes`

Required:

- `json_path` (String) Simple JSON Path which is evaluated against custom claims of the [OIDC ID Token](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken) to produce the value of the field. E.g., `.custom_attributes`, `.google.compute_engine`, etc. JSON Path must start either from `.` or from `//goessner.net/articles/JsonPath/






<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--deregistration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.deregistration`

Optional:

- `propagation_delay` (Attributes) Estimated amount of time it takes to propagate the unregistration event across all affected mesh nodes. During this time interval affected proxies will continue making requests to the deregistered workload until the respective configuration update arrives. To prevent traffic loss, `Workload Onboarding Agent` SHOULD delay shutdown of the the workload's sidecar for that time period. As a rule of thumb, this value should remain relatively small, e.g. under 15 seconds. The reason for this is that shutdown flow on the workload's side is time-boxed. E.g., on VMs there is a stop timeout enforced by SystemD, while on AWS ECS there is a stop timeout enforced by ECS Agent. If you pick a delay value that is too big, `Workload Onboarding Agent` will delay shutdown of the sidecar for too long; as a result sidecar risks to get terminated abruptly instead of graceful connection draining. Defaults to `10s`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--deregistration--propagation_delay))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--onboarding--workloads--deregistration--propagation_delay"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.onboarding.workloads.deregistration.propagation_delay`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years





<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability`

Optional:

- `agent` (Attributes) Settings for the `SPM Agent`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--agent))
- `domains` (List of String) List of domains to consider when discovering services. Services outside of domains specified here will NOT be added to the registry of services known to TSB and will NOT appear on TSB UI. Empty list means that discovered services may belong to any domain. Defaults to an empty list.
- `enabled` (Attributes) To install the component,  set the value to `true`. Defaults to `false`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--enabled))
- `service_discovery` (Attributes) Configure `Tetrate Service Discovery` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--agent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.agent`

Optional:

- `version` (String) Version of the `SPM Agent` to use. Defaults to the version of the `SPM Agent` included into a given TSB release.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--enabled"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.enabled`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery`

Optional:

- `agent` (Attributes) Settings for the `Tetrate Service Discovery Agent`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--agent))
- `plane` (Attributes) Configure `Tetrate Service Discovery Plane` component. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--agent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.agent`

Optional:

- `version` (String) Version of the `Tetrate Service Discovery Agent` to use. Defaults to the version of the `Tetrate Service Discovery Agent` included into a given TSB release.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane`

Optional:

- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec))
- `log_levels` (Map of String) The log level configuration by scopes. Supported log levels: "none", "error", "info", "debug".

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>

- `node_port` (Number)

- `protocol` (String)
- `target_port` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_expansion.service_observability.service_discovery.plane.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_expansion--service_observability--service_discovery--plane--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.








<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_observability"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_observability`

Optional:

- `demo_settings` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_observability--demo_settings))
- `settings` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_observability--settings))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_observability--demo_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_observability.demo_settings`

Optional:

- `api_endpoint_metrics_enabled` (Boolean) Toggle to process, analyze, and generate api endpoints RED metrics. By default `false` which means disabled. If you want to analyze all your request and generate RED metrics for each endpoint of your registered services in the mesh, set it to `true`.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--mesh_observability--settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.mesh_observability.settings`

Optional:

- `api_endpoint_metrics_enabled` (Boolean) Toggle to process, analyze, and generate api endpoints RED metrics. By default `false` which means disabled. If you want to analyze all your request and generate RED metrics for each endpoint of your registered services in the mesh, set it to `true`.



<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.provider_settings`

Optional:

- `aws` (Attributes) Global settings to AWS. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--aws))
- `eks` (Attributes) Settings specific to EKS. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--eks))
- `lattice` (Attributes) Settings specific to Lattice. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--lattice))
- `route53` (Attributes) Settings specific to Route53. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53))

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--aws"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.provider_settings.aws`

Required:

- `service_account_name` (String) Service account name to use with IAM role association. Required. This service account should have the proper permissions depending on which AWS services are enabled. (Route53, Lattice, etc.)


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--eks"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.provider_settings.eks`

Optional:

- `use_nlb_by_default` (Boolean) When true, gateways will be configured to use NLBs with cross zone load balancing enabled when the load balancer type is not configured. When false, no additional annotations will be added.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--lattice"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.provider_settings.lattice`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable/disable the Lattice integration controller. Default: false.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.provider_settings.route53`

Required:

- `service_account_name` (String) Service account name to use for IAM role. Required. Deprecated, user AWSIntegrationSettings instead.

Optional:

- `domain_filter` (List of String) List of domains to limit possible target zones by a domain suffix. Default is empty list with means consider all resources as DNS target.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable/disable the Route53 integration controller. Default: false.
- `evaluate_target_health` (Attributes) Control whether to evaluate the health of a DNS target. Default: true. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53--evaluate_target_health))
- `filter_settings` (Attributes) Filter target settings. It filters out (removes) targets that matches any of the filters. Optional. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53--filter_settings))
- `interval` (Attributes) Duration of interval between individual synchronizations. Default: 60s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53--interval))
- `namespace_selector` (Attributes) Specifies the namespace to watch. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53--namespace_selector))
- `policy` (String) Specifies the policy to use when managing DNS records. Default: SYNC.
- `ttl` (Number) Default TTL (in seconds) value for DNS records. Default: 300.

<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53--evaluate_target_health"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.provider_settings.route53.evaluate_target_health`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53--filter_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.provider_settings.route53.filter_settings`

Optional:

- `annotation_filter` (String) Filter out (remove) targets that matches annotation using label selector semantics. Optional. *NOTE*: The annotation value currently cannot be longer thant 63 characters.
- `exclude_domain` (List of String) Exclude subdomains. Optional.
- `label_filter` (String) Filter out (remove) targets that matches label selector. Optional.
- `zone_id_filter` (List of String) When using the AWS provider, filter for zones with this ID. Optional.
- `zone_tag_filter` (List of String) When using the AWS provider, filter for zones with this tag. Optional, format: key=value.
- `zone_type` (String) Filter out (removes) zones of this type. Default: none, options: none, public, private.


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53--interval"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.provider_settings.route53.interval`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--install_template--helm--spec--provider_settings--route53--namespace_selector"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install_template.helm.spec.provider_settings.route53.namespace_selector`

Optional:

- `ignore_namespaces` (String) Comma separated list of namespaces to ignore when watching for DNS endpoints. When using this option remember to include the name of the namespace in which Control Plane is installed. If Management Plane is installed in the same cluster include the namespace name in this option as well. Mutually exclusive with `namespace`. Default: the namespace where the controller is running, usually `istio-system`.
- `namespace` (String) Specifies the namespace to watch for resources. Mutually exclusive with `ignore_namespaces`. If not specified (""), all namespaces will be watched which is the default.







<a id="nestedatt--locality"></a>
### Nested Schema for `locality`

Required:

- `region` (String) The geographic location of the cluster.


<a id="nestedatt--namespace_scope"></a>
### Nested Schema for `namespace_scope`

Optional:

- `exceptions` (List of String) Namespaces to be excluded form the default scope. If the scope is set to global, this list will contain namespaces that are considered local. If the scope is set to local, this list will contain namespaces that are considered global.
- `scope` (String) Default scope for namespaces in this cluster (global, local)


<a id="nestedatt--namespaces"></a>
### Nested Schema for `namespaces`

Optional:

- `istio` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--namespaces--istio))
- `labels` (Map of String) The Labels of the namespace.
- `name` (String)
- `services` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--namespaces--services))

<a id="nestedatt--namespaces--istio"></a>
### Nested Schema for `namespaces.istio`

Optional:

- `istio_injection` (String) Istio injection status for the namespace.
- `istio_revision` (String) Istio revision of the namespace.


<a id="nestedatt--namespaces--services"></a>
### Nested Schema for `namespaces.services`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Annotations associated with the service
- `canonical_name` (String) The canonical name of the service defined by labels (value of service.istio.io/canonical-name)
- `gateway_host` (Boolean) Indicates that this is a service representing a gateway host (used for multi-cluster scenarios).
- `hostname` (String) The hostname by which this service is accessed. Can correspond to the hostname of an internal service or that of a virtual host on a gateway.
- `kubernetes_external_addresses` (List of String) For kubernetes services of type load balancer, this field contains the list of lb hostnames or IPs assigned to the service. For services of type nodePort, this field contains the IP addresses of the nodes in the cluster.
- `kubernetes_service_fqdn` (String) If this is a gateway host service, this field will indicate the kubernetes service that is running as a gateway and exposing this host to the outside world.
- `kubernetes_service_ip` (String) For services running on Kubernetes, the cluster IP of the service in the cluster.
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels associated with the service
- `mesh_external` (Boolean) indicates that this is an external service (service entry MESH_EXTERNAL location)
- `name` (String) Name assigned to the service
- `namespace` (String) namespace associated with the service.
- `num_hops` (Number) The number of hops from the advertising cluster to the ingress gateway that is exposing the gateway host. If the advertiser is the cluster owning the ingress gateway host, the hops is 0. If the advertiser is a tier1 cluster exposing gateways via internal or external Servers, the path hops is 1.
- `num_kubernetes_endpoints` (Number) The number of kubernetes pods providing this service. Note that a service could be hosted on both pods and VMs.
- `num_vm_endpoints` (Number) The number of VMs providing this service.
- `ports` (Attributes List) The set of ports on which this service is exposed. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--namespaces--services--ports))
- `selector` (Map of String) label selectors associated with the service.
- `spiffe_ids` (List of String) List of SPIFFE identities used by the workloads of the service.
- `state` (String) State of the Service(External/Observed/Controlled)
- `subsets` (List of String) Name of subsets defined for this service
- `tier1_gateway_host` (Boolean) Indicates that this is a service representing a gateway host acting as a tier1 gateway. This field is being introduced specifically for the hostnames exposed through Tier1Gateway's ExternalServers. Fo all other hostnames that are exposed exposed on IngessGateways or Tier1Gateway's InternalServers gateway_host field is set to true because that is used for multicluster routing setup. For ExternalServer hostnames multicluster cluster routing is not setup and hence UI on finding gateway_host as false shows these hostnames as internal services incorrectly. To help UI, this field is being introduced. UI would look for either gateway_host or tier1_gateway_host to decide if the hostname is a gateway hostname.
- `workloads` (Attributes List) Workloads implementing the Service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--namespaces--services--workloads))

<a id="nestedatt--namespaces--services--ports"></a>
### Nested Schema for `namespaces.services.ports`

Optional:

- `kubernetes_node_port` (Number) Indicates the node port attached to a physical deployment on a kubernetes cluster.
- `name` (String) Name assigned to the port.
- `number` (Number) A valid non-negative integer port number.


<a id="nestedatt--namespaces--services--workloads"></a>
### Nested Schema for `namespaces.services.workloads`

Optional:

- `address` (String) Routable address of the workload.
- `is_vm` (Boolean) Indicates whether the workload is kubernetes endpoint or vm.
- `name` (String) Instance name of the workload.
- `proxy` (Attributes) Proxy details. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--namespaces--services--workloads--proxy))

<a id="nestedatt--namespaces--services--workloads--proxy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `namespaces.services.workloads.proxy`

Optional:

- `control_plane_address` (String) Address/service of control plane entity controlling the proxy like istiod.istio-system.svc:15012.
- `envoy_version` (String) Envoy version of the proxy.
- `istio_version` (String) Istio version of the proxy.
- `status` (Map of String) Sync status for each xDS component. For example: status["CDS"] = "SYNCED" XDS components are: LDS, RDS, EDS CDS and SRDS. Refer to Envoy go-control-plane ConfigStatus for possible status values values: https://github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/blob/main/envoy/service/status/v3/csds.pb.go





<a id="nestedatt--state"></a>
### Nested Schema for `state`

Optional:

- `discovered_locality` (Attributes) Discovered locality is the locality/region of the cluster as discovered by the xcp from the k8s endpoints (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--state--discovered_locality))
- `istio_revisions` (Attributes List) Metadata of different Istio revision found in the cluster. An empty istio revisions field represents there was no Istio discovered in the cluster. Field should not be empty in ControlMode as TSB will install and depend on Istio. In Observe mode, an empty field represents that a vanilla kubernetes cluster. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--state--istio_revisions))
- `istio_versions` (List of String) This shows currently running istio versions in the cluster.
- `last_sync_time` (Attributes) last time xcp edge(cp) synced with central(mp) in the UTC format (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--state--last_sync_time))
- `mode` (String) Mode in which the  Control Plane is deployed.
- `provider` (String) cluster provider. Ex: GKE, EKS, AKS
- `tsb_cp_version` (String) TSB controlplane version
- `xcp_version` (String) xcp-edge version which is running at the cluster

<a id="nestedatt--state--discovered_locality"></a>
### Nested Schema for `state.discovered_locality`

Required:

- `region` (String) The geographic location of the cluster.


<a id="nestedatt--state--istio_revisions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `state.istio_revisions`

Optional:

- `distribution` (String) Istio distribution found in the cluster.
- `revision` (String) Istio revision found in the cluster
- `version` (String) Istio version found in the cluster.


<a id="nestedatt--state--last_sync_time"></a>
### Nested Schema for `state.last_sync_time`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Non-negative fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution. Negative second values with fractions must still have non-negative nanos values that count forward in time. Must be from 0 to 999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Represents seconds of UTC time since Unix epoch 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z. Must be from 0001-01-01T00:00:00Z to 9999-12-31T23:59:59Z inclusive.



<a id="nestedatt--tier1_cluster"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tier1_cluster`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--token_ttl"></a>
### Nested Schema for `token_ttl`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years
